# Figure description: скв_114.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_114.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (каротажная диаграмма)** — a composite well log chart typical of Soviet-era petroleum and mineral exploration practice. It combines lithological/stratigraphic columns on the left with multiple geophysical logging curves in the central panels, plus core description and sample data columns on the right. The format is a standard Soviet геолого-геофизический разрез по скважине (geological-geophysical borehole section).

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure is titled **"ДИАГРАММЫ КАРОТАЖА"** (Wireline/Geophysical Logging Diagrams). It appears to cover a single borehole (скважина), though the precise well number is difficult to read at this resolution. The log appears to span a depth range of approximately **0 to ~130 metres** (based on the left-hand depth scale visible along the margin), with a supplementary inset panel at the bottom showing an additional depth interval or repeat section (possibly ~80–100 m range). The survey is associated with the northern Kazakhstan regional mapping project (map sheets M-42/N-42 context).

---

## Key Content

The figure is organized into the following major column groups, left to right:

### Left Section — "ПО БУРЕНИЮ" (Drilling Data):
- **Depth scale** (numbered in metres, left margin, approximately every 10 m from surface to ~130 m)
- **Литологическая колонка** (Lithological column): A graphical strip log showing layered geology using standard Soviet lithological symbols — horizontal dashes (clay/mudstone), brick patterns (sandstone/limestone), dotted patterns (sand/gravel), and cross-hatched zones (possibly crystalline or altered rock). The upper ~20 m shows relatively uniform fine-grained material; the middle section (roughly 20–80 m) shows interbedded coarser and finer units with notable heterogeneity; the lower section (80–130 m) appears more massive or coarser-grained.
- **Описание пород** (Rock description column): Narrow columns with written lithological descriptions (too small to read fully at this resolution).
- Additional narrow columns for drilling parameters (possibly core recovery, RQD, or sample intervals).

### Central Section — "ПО КАРОТАЖУ" (Geophysical Logging):
Multiple geophysical curves are plotted against the same depth scale. Based on Soviet logging convention and the column headers visible, these include:

- **КС (КВН/БК или аналог)** — Electrical resistivity log (кажущееся сопротивление, КС), shown as a curve deflecting left and right. Several sharp high-resistivity anomalies are visible, particularly around 55–65 m depth, suggesting dense or mineralized rock. The curve label reads something like **КС Б-1, 0.45 мка/у** or similar electrode configuration notation.
- **ПС (самопроизвольная поляризация)** — Spontaneous Potential (SP) log: a smoother curve showing electrochemical contrasts between formations, useful for identifying permeable beds.
- **ДС (диаметр скважины / кавернометрия)** — Caliper log showing borehole diameter variations — useful for identifying washed-out zones (clays/unconsolidated material) vs. competent rock.
- **ГК (гамма-каротаж)** — Gamma-ray log: visible as a separate curve, showing natural radioactivity variations. Elevated gamma values correlate with clay-rich or uranium-bearing intervals.
- A second panel to the right of the main curves appears to show **НГК (нейтронный гамма-каротаж)** — Neutron-gamma log, useful for porosity/fluid estimation.
- There is a notably **large anomalous deflection** in one of the resistivity or induced polarization curves at approximately 55–65 m depth, which may indicate a mineralized zone, dense intrusive rock, or a significant lithological contrast.

### Right Section — "РЕЗУЛЬТАТЫ КАРОТАЖНОГО АНАЛИЗА" (Logging Interpretation Results):
- A secondary lithological column (possibly from core or cuttings)
- **Примечания** (Remarks/Annotations) column
- A table of numerical values in the far right columns, with headers suggesting: **Ra** (possibly radium or radon), **Rн**, **Сп**, **Да**, **Rд**, **Да** — likely interpreted formation parameters such as true resistivity, SP amplitude, and bed thickness corrections
- Numerical values appear in rows corresponding to identified intervals of interest

### Bottom Inset:
A smaller repeat section or supplementary log panel labeled **"К АНАЛИЗУ КАВЕРНОГРАММЫ"** (For Caliper Analysis) or similar, showing curves over a reduced depth interval — possibly a detail of the most anomalous zone.
